page: Amazon Web Services r8ib.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance belonging to the r8ib server family. It is powered by dedicated Intel processors with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance features 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, managed via the Nitro hypervisor.

This server is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8ib.2xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio suitable for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of local storage or GPU accelerators. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics.
description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores, with 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, focusing its resource allocation entirely on memory capacity and processing power. Network performance is anchored by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration. The r8ib.2xlarge is suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
